rationale: The citing paper uses Dubois, Bonezzi, and De Angelis (2016) in its theory-building section to justify a specific behavioral claim: self-enhancement is stronger with weaker ties than stronger ties. That claim is then used to predict a core differential effect in the model, namely why common followers should matter more than common mutual followers for sharing. This is more than a background mention because it informs hypothesis logic and interpretation of results.
relation_description: The citing paper applies a behavioral insight from the cited WOM study (tie strength shapes self-enhancement motives) to explain and predict differential effects of follower-type overlap on social media sharing propensity. The cited work functions as conceptual support for the weak-tie versus strong-tie mechanism in the citing paper's framework.
